actionable_feedback: Add a short reproducible script or spreadsheet formula snippet (e.g., a 6-12 line Python or Excel formula block) to QC_memo.md so an auditor can re-run the exact calculations.
Add an explicit 'normalized_value' (or 'blank_corrected_value') column header in normalized_assay_table.csv to match common schema conventions and improve interoperability.
Complete any truncated sections in QC_memo.md (the preview shows the file was truncated) so the memo includes full exceptions/appendices referenced in the header.
In the memo, include a brief reference or pointer to the laboratory SOP that would be used to implement dilution recommendations (or note that SOP is required if unavailable).
Optionally expand uncertainty discussion with one or two example replicate/noise scenarios to illustrate how close-margin cases (e.g., the 0.02 blank excess) might change decisions.

feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables meet the artifact contract and pass deterministic checks. Calculations (blank correction, delta comparisons, dilution arithmetic) are correct and traceable to the packet values. Plate-level QC was applied correctly (blank 0.10 > cutoff 0.08 => invalidate plate). Documentation clearly states assumptions, uncertainty caveats, and recommended laboratory review. Verifier noted a minor non-file evidence_ref string in grader evidence_refs, but this did not affect grounding or scores.

extra_model_fields.notable_verifier_note: Minor inconsistency: one grader evidence_ref string was generic ('artifact_content_previews (provided in grader input)') rather than a concrete artifact path. No hidden-reference leak detected.
